perturbation.original_text: Sponsor will be the exclusive retail music store sponsor of the Excite Site and the Excite Broadcast Pages. Excite will not permit the display of advertising banners, promotional buttons, promotional links or other promotional materials for any retail sale of Music Products on the Excite Site, except those Music Products offered by Sponsor, nor advertising by any other Retail Music Store. "Retail Music Store" shall mean any entity which is primarily engaged in the sale of Music Products at retail to consumers. "Music Products" 2 CONFIDENTIAL shall mean pre-recorded music hard goods, digitally distributed music (except live and/or cybercast events), music books (to the extent this Agreement does not conflict with Excite's existing agreement with Amazon.com), music-related t-shirts and apparel, and music videos (except live and/or cybercast events). Notwithstanding the foregoing, Excite may make available opportunities on the Excite Site to purchase Music Products from parties other than Sponsor if such Music Products are not available from Sponsor so long as, prior to entering into arrangements to make available opportunities to purchase Music Products from parties other than Sponsor, Excite notifies Sponsor of its interest in the Music Products and gives Sponsor thirty (30) days to make the desired Music Products available through the Sponsor Site. Excite and Sponsor acknowledge that certain market opportunities may arise in which the desired Music Products must be made available on less than thirty (30) days advance notice and will work together in good faith to maximize those opportunities. In no event will Excite enter into arrangements to make available opportunities to purchase Music Products from parties other than Sponsor that would prevent Sponsor from being the exclusive source of such Music Products on the Excite Site once the Music Products become available through Sponsor.
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsor will be the exclusive retail music store sponsor of the Excite Site and the Excite Broadcast Pages. Excite will not permit the display of advertising banners, promotional buttons, promotional links or other promotional materials for any retail sale of Music Products on the Excite Site, except those Music Products offered by Sponsor, nor advertising by any other Retail Music Store. "Retail Music Store" shall mean any entity which is primarily engaged in the sale of Music Products at retail to consumers. "Music Products" 2 CONFIDENTIAL shall mean pre-recorded music hard goods, digitally distributed music (except live and/or cybercast events), music books, music-related t-shirts and apparel, and music videos. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Excite may make available opportunities on the Excite Site to purchase Music Products from parties other than Sponsor if such Music Products are not available from Sponsor so long as, prior to entering into arrangements to make available opportunities to purchase Music Products from parties other than Sponsor, Excite notifies Sponsor of its interest in the Music Products and gives Sponsor thirty (30) days to make the desired Music Products available through the Sponsor Site. Excite and Sponsor acknowledge that certain market opportunities may arise in which the desired Music Products must be made available on less than thirty (30) days advance notice and will work together in good faith to maximize those opportunities. In no event will Excite enter into arrangements to make available opportunities to purchase Music Products from parties other than Sponsor that would prevent Sponsor from being the exclusive source of such Music Products on the Excite Site once the Music Products become available through Sponsor.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the parenthetical exception "(to the extent this Agreement does not conflict with Excite's existing agreement with Amazon.com)" from the definition of 'Music Products,' this creates a direct conflict with Excite's ability to honor its pre-existing agreement with Amazon.com, causing uncertainty on which agreement takes precedence on the definition of Music Products. This potentially impacts the exclusivity clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sponsor will be the exclusive retail music store sponsor of the Excite Site and the Excite Broadcast Pages. Excite will not permit the display of advertising banners, promotional buttons, promotional links or other promotional materials for any retail sale of Music Products on the Excite Site, except those Music Products offered by Sponsor, nor advertising by any other Retail Music Store. "Retail Music Store" shall mean any entity which is primarily engaged in the sale of Music Products at retail to consumers. "Music Products" 2 CONFIDENTIAL shall mean pre-recorded music hard goods, digitally distributed music (except live and/or cybercast events), music books, music-related t-shirts and apparel, and music videos. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Excite may make available opportunities on the Excite Site to purchase Music Products from parties other than Sponsor if such Music Products are not available from Sponsor so long as, prior to entering into arrangements to make available opportunities to purchase Music Products from parties other than Sponsor, Excite notifies Sponsor of its interest in the Music Products and gives Sponsor thirty (30) days to make the desired Music Products available through the Sponsor Site. Excite and Sponsor acknowledge that certain market opportunities may arise in which the desired Music Products must be made available on less than thirty (30) days advance notice and will work together in good faith to maximize those opportunities. In no event will Excite enter into arrangements to make available opportunities to purchase Music Products from parties other than Sponsor that would prevent Sponsor from being the exclusive source of such Music Products on the Excite Site once the Music Products become available through Sponsor.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the parenthetical exception '(to the extent this Agreement does not conflict with Excite's existing agreement with Amazon.com)' from the definition of 'Music Products' creates a direct conflict because it now includes music books without regard to the Amazon agreement. This contradicts the original intention of the agreement, as Excite may now be in breach of its agreement with Amazon if it allows Sponsor to be the exclusive provider of music books that are already under contract with Amazon. This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding the scope of the exclusivity clause and Excite's obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Sponsor will pay Excite [****] per year as compensation for being the exclusive online retail music store sponsor of the Excite Site. 7 8 CONFIDENTIAL c) Sponsor will pay Excite [****] in the first year of the term of the Agreement as compensation for on-going programming, links, placements, advertisements and promotions contemplated by this Agreement. d) Sponsor will pay Excite [****] in the second year of the term of the Agreement as compensation for on-going programming, links, placements, advertisements and promotions contemplated by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsor will pay Excite [****] per year as compensation for being the exclusive online retail music store sponsor of the Excite Site. 7 8 CONFIDENTIAL c) Sponsor will pay Excite [****] in the first year of the term of the Agreement as compensation for on-going programming. d) Sponsor will pay Excite [****] in the second year of the term of the Agreement as compensation for on-going programming.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the words "links, placements, advertisements and promotions contemplated by this Agreement." from sections 7(c) and 7(d), the agreement becomes vague and it is no longer clear if the links, placements, advertisements and promotions are contemplated. This can cause uncertainty on the agreement and cause it to become difficult to be enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sponsor will pay Excite [****] in the first year of the term of the Agreement as compensation for on-going programming.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the scope of compensation by removing 'links, placements, advertisements and promotions contemplated by this Agreement.' While this creates ambiguity and potential for dispute, it doesn't create a direct, clear contradiction with the contradicted text which simply states Sponsor will pay Excite [****] in the first year of the term of the Agreement as compensation for on-going programming. The contradicted text can still be interpreted as being true after the changed text and therefore does not cause a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All data concerning users and their behavior relating to the use of the Excite Site will be owned solely by Excite. Data relating to the use of Sponsor's Site (excluding users referred from the Excite Site) will be owned solely by Sponsor. Data relating to the use of the Co-Branded Pages will be jointly owned and shared by both parties. b) All jointly owned and shared data will be held in confidence and will not be used except in accordance with reasonable guidelines to be mutually agreed upon by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: All data concerning users and their behavior relating to the use of the Excite Site will be owned solely by Excite. Data relating to the use of Sponsor's Site will be owned solely by Sponsor. Data relating to the use of the Co-Branded Pages will be jointly owned and shared by both parties. b) All jointly owned and shared data will be held in confidence and will not be used except in accordance with reasonable guidelines to be mutually agreed upon by the parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause "(excluding users referred from the Excite Site)" from the section, it creates an in-text contradiction between the data owned by the Excite and the data owned by the Sponsor. Creating an uncertainty on which entity owns the data from the users reffered to the Excite site
perturbation.contradicted_text: All data concerning users and their behavior relating to the use of the Excite Site will be owned solely by Excite. Data relating to the use of Sponsor's Site will be owned solely by Sponsor. Data relating to the use of the Co-Branded Pages will be jointly owned and shared by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The removal of '(excluding users referred from the Excite Site)' in the Changed Text directly contradicts the original ownership stipulations outlined in both the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text. Initially, the Sponsor solely owned data from its site, explicitly excluding users referred from Excite. Now, without this exclusion, there is ambiguity concerning the ownership of data from Excite-referred users on the Sponsor's site. This uncertainty creates a direct conflict regarding which party owns or has rights to that specific user data, thus creating an in-text contradiction according to the provided 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition, where essential language is removed, creating a contradiction.
